stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The League of United Latin American Citizens (LULAC) was founded in Corpus Christi, Texas, in 1929 by representatives of several Mexican American civic organizations.
  • LULAC was established to advance the civil rights of Latino communities in the United States.
  • Ben Garza served as LULAC's first national president.
  • In 1948, LULAC supported the legal team in Delgado v. Bastrop ISD, a federal case challenging school segregation of Mexican American students in Texas.
  • The court's ruling required Texas to end its practice of segregating Mexican American students.
stem: The student wants to identify an accomplishment of LULAC. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: LULAC, founded by representatives of several Mexican American civic organizations, had Ben Garza as its first national president.
LULAC's support of the legal team in Delgado v. Bastrop ISD was key to ending the segregation of Mexican American students in Texas public schools.
Founded in 1929 in Corpus Christi, Texas, LULAC was created by representatives of several Mexican American civic organizations.
In 1948, LULAC initiated the practice of ending segregation of Mexican American students in Texas public schools.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because it identifies a concrete accomplishment of LULAC—its decisive role in the Delgado case ending school segregation of Mexican American students.

Choices A and C describe LULAC's founding but no accomplishment. Choice D overstates LULAC's role.
